game_name: Spider-Man: Return of the Sinister Six

design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through side-scrolling levels, defeat six Sinister Six bosses sequentially, collect web fluid and occasional key items to advance
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Strict resource scarcity (single life, one continue, no health restoration items) combined with imprecise combat mechanics requiring careful enemy spacing
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Combat Spider-Man against iconic Marvel villains with web-based mechanics and climbing abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Punishing lives system with minimal checkpointing reflects early 90s arcade-influenced design; weak play control is a defining friction point that shapes the challenge entirely

difficulty.lives_system: Single life per continue; player starts with one life and one continue total
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-level checkpoints; death returns to level start or game over
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Master System version reduces difficulty by relocating items to easier positions, removing enemies, redesigning jumps, and reducing boss health regeneration phases; Game Gear version increases difficulty through reduced screen visibility

progression.upgrade_system: Web fluid collection enables square web projectile attacks; defeated enemies restore Spider-Man's power bar incrementally
progression.unlock_mechanism: Key items and detonators found within levels gate progression; six sequential boss encounters unlock next stage
replayability_hooks: Master System port offers reduced difficulty for accessibility
Game Gear port offers increased difficulty through screen constraint
Pattern learning on boss encounters (Electro, Sandman, Mysterio, Vulture, Hobgoblin, Doctor Octopus)

world_building_techniques: Loosely adapted from The Amazing Spider-Man story arc #334-339
Doctor Octopus framed as master planner orchestrating Sinister Six takeover plot
Level themes implicitly reflect villain characteristics (e.g., Electro's electrical stage design)

boss_design.philosophy: Single-encounter villain gauntlet with increasing complexity; each Sinister Six member represents distinct combat challenge requiring pattern recognition and precise execution
notable_bosses: Mysterio - stage 3 (regenerates health multiple times in NES version; single health bar in Master System)
Doctor Octopus - final boss (regenerates health in NES version; single health bar in Master System)

danger_signals: World conquest plot by organized villain collective
environmental_storytelling: Stage theming tied to individual villain abilities
Item placement (keys, detonators) suggests sabotage/infiltration scenarios

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Spider-Man: Return of the Sinister Six NES)
wikipedia - Spider-Man: Return of the Sinister Six
